programme.title: Danger Zone
programme.desc: Rick Morgan (Billy Zane), an American engineer who runs a mine in East Africa, is approached one day by his friend Jim Scott (Robert Downey Jr.), who needs someplace to store canisters of toxic waste. Unfortunately, a spill occurs a short time later, resulting in deaths and Morgan having to leave the country. A year later, he is approached by a government agent (Ron Silver) looking for a missing supply of potentially hazardous poison, which in truth hides something even more valuable.

programme.title: Arlington Road [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Widowed when his FBI agent wife is killed by an extremist group, college professor Michael Faraday (Jeff Bridges) becomes obsessed with the culture of these groups -- especially when his new all-American neighbors, Oliver (Tim Robbins) and Cheryl Lang (Joan Cusack), start acting suspiciously. With each twist, the mystery deepens and questions loom whether Faraday is consumed by fear and driven by paranoia, or whether a lethal conspiracy has been born on Arlington Road.

programme.title: Just Getting Started
programme.desc: Duke Diver is living the high life as the freewheeling manager of a luxurious resort in Palm Springs, Calif. He soon faces competition from Leo, a former military man who likes the same woman that Duke is interested in. When Diver's past suddenly catches up with him, he must put aside his differences and reluctantly team up with Leo to stop whoever is trying to kill him.

programme.title: The Code
programme.desc: A veteran thief recruits a younger crook to help him pull off one final job in order to repay his debt to the Russian mob.

programme.title: FernGully: The Last Rainforest
programme.desc: The magical inhabitants of a rainforest fight to save their home, which is threatened by logging

programme.title: The Nut Job
programme.desc: An incorrigibly self-serving exiled squirrel finds himself helping his former park brethren survive by raiding a nut store, a location that also happens to be a front for a human gang's bank robbery.

programme.title: The Nut Job 2: Nutty by Nature
programme.desc: When the evil mayor of Oakton decides to bulldoze Liberty Park and build a dangerous amusement park in its place, Surly Squirrel and his ragtag group of animal friends need to band together to save their home, defeat the mayor, and take back the park.

programme.title: Point Break
programme.desc: An F.B.I. Agent goes undercover to catch a gang of surfers who may be bank robbers

programme.title: Murphy's Romance
programme.desc: Emma moves to a ranch after a divorce and befriends the older Murphy, but then her ex shows up.

programme.title: Aloha
programme.desc: In Aloha, a celebrated military contractor (Bradley Cooper) returns to the site of his greatest career triumphs – the US Space program in Honolulu, Hawaii – and reconnects with a long-ago love (Rachel McAdams) while unexpectedly falling for the hard-charging Air Force watchdog (Emma Stone) assigned to him. From Academy Award-winner Cameron Crowe, the writer-director behind such films as Jerry Maguire and Almost Famous, Aloha also stars Bill Murray, John Krasinski, Danny McBride, and Alec Baldwin.

programme.title: The Monuments Men
programme.desc: In the midst of WWII a small group of art experts are commissioned by roosevelt as soldiers and sent to the european theater to protect historically important buildings and monuments, and to locate vast troves of priceless art looted by the nazis. Discovering art in castles and mines, they race against time as the nazis scramble to destroy everything they can.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Ninja
programme.desc: SATURDAY NIGHT LIVE veteran Chris Farley stars as an orphan who was raised from infancy by the head of a ninja institute. Farley grows into an overweight young klutz who manages to become a ninja, albeit and inept one. His first assignment is not to screw up a murder investigation in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Collector
programme.desc: A mild-mannered bank teller and butterfly collector kidnaps the object of his affections, a young art student, for his collection.

programme.title: Born Yesterday
programme.desc: A million dollar Tycoon hires a tutor to teach his lover proper etiquette.

programme.title: To Sir, With Love

programme.title: Easy Rider
programme.desc: A landmark road film, EASY RIDER chronicles the search for freedom by two motorcycle-riding drifters (Peter Fonda and Dennis Hopper) who meet up with alcoholic lawyer, Jack Nicholson in a southern jail. Nicholson gets them out and then joins them on their liberating journey.

programme.title: The Lawnmower Man (Theatrical Cut)
programme.desc: Dr. Lawrence Angelo (Pierce Brosnan) is a brilliant scientist obsessed with perfecting virtual reality software. When his experiments on animals fail, he finds the ideal substitute – Jobe Smith (Jeff Fahey), a slow-witted gardener. Dr. Angelo's goal is to benefit his human guinea pig and ultimately mankind itself, but evil lurks the guise of "the Shop," a shadowy group that seeks to use the technology to create an invincible war machine. When the experiments change the simple Jobe into a superhuman being, the stage is set for a Jekyll-and-Hyde struggle for the control of Jobe's mind and the future of the world.

programme.title: Judgment Night
programme.desc: Four young friends, while taking a shortcut en route to a local boxing match, witness a brutal murder which leaves them running for their lives.

programme.title: Unlawful Entry
programme.desc: A burglar holds a knife to Karen's throat while her husband does nothing. The couple ends up befriending the cop that comes. The friendship ends when the cop beats up the culprit. Karen isn't ready to end it. Things get ugly with the cop.

programme.title: Emmet Otter's Jug-Band Christmas
programme.desc: This classic Christmas story is about Ma and Emmet Otter - a family that has little more than each other. programme.desc: PR exec Mikayla strikes a deal with pastry chef Austin to rebrand his café to get dating advice.

programme.title: Love at the Bootcamp
programme.desc: A scattered marketing exec helps rebrand a bootcamp and discovers its strict owner may be just what she needs.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Former Marine John "Falcon" Chapman is a dark anti-hero driven by guilt and suffering from PTSD, who will destroy himself unless given something else to destroy - a useful weapon-of-last-resort for the U.S. State Department.

programme.title: Traffickers
programme.desc: Tiffany Cooper, disgraced by the military, wanted by the cops and running out of time, stops at nothing to take down a Trafficking Operation and save these young girls. Nothing is what it seems when everyone seems to be against you. Trust No One.

programme.title: On Angel's Wings
programme.desc: When an angel grants a struggling water girl (Taylor Faye Ruffin) three wishes, even her mom (Robin Givens) or coach (Reginald VelJohnson) can't figure out how she became a soccer superstar.

programme.title: Murder Without Motive
programme.desc: Edmund Perry has everything going for him after he graduates with a scholarship to attend Stanford. With his dedication paying off, an encounter with a police officer senselessly ends his life.

programme.title: The Struggle
programme.desc: A story about Debra and her daughter, Tracey struggling in the heart of Memphis. Debra tries to keep her daughter out of danger and off the streets but it doesn't help with the surrounding of drug dealers, pimps and prostitution. It gets worst when Erin and her gang interfere in situations with Tracey and Kandi. That's when Tracey takes matters in her own hands.

programme.title: The Struggle 2: The Dilemma
programme.desc: Darnell gets released from jail and tries to take back control of his territory. Now that Tracey has left and moved to Atlanta with her mom...her sister Stacey is seeking revenge.

programme.title: The Private Secretary
programme.desc: Pamela and Sterling are the married owners of a thriving tech empire, where they have all the power and money in the world. As the demands of their business have grown, they bring on Vanessa, a high efficient and enigmatic private secretary. What initially seems as a godsend, Vanessa soon begins to dive further into their lives, and has an agenda that could bring their empire to ruin.

programme.title: The Only Child
programme.desc: Twelve-year old Chris has a wonderful and thriving childhood with her loving family. When her mother dies tragically in a car accident, she begins to go down a dark spiral. When Chris’ father starts seeing another woman, she begins a sinister plan, as no woman will ever replace her mother.

programme.title: Meli
programme.desc: After finding an eviction notice on her door, Meli decides it’s time to up the ante and take matters into her own hands to protect her family by any means necessary. Out of pure desperation she attempts to burglarize a stranger’s luxurious home.

programme.title: Triassic World
programme.desc: Dinosaurs used to grow human organs for transplants escape. The scientists that created them must fight back.

programme.title: War of the Worlds 2: The Next Wave
programme.desc: The aliens return to complete their plan of human domination, launching a second attack to destroy humanity.

programme.title: Independents' Day
programme.desc: Aliens invade Earth, tell humans to emigrate to a new planet or die. The President and a militia fight back.

programme.title: Super Cyclone
programme.desc: 300 MPH winds threaten America, so a lone meteorologist must battle the elements to stop the threat.

programme.title: Thor: God of Thunder
programme.desc: Loki escapes Asgard to gain dominion of Earth. Thor must stop him before it’s too late.

programme.title: Nightbeast
programme.desc: An alien with an appetite for blood and disembowelment is set loose on a small town when its spacecraft crashes and explodes. When sheriff Jack Cinder and the local militia arrive on scene to investigate, a violent battle ensues, leaving only Jack, his sensuous lady deputy and a wild-eyed redneck and survivors. With a corrupt mayor unwilling to stand up against the Nightbeast's reign of carnage and destruction, it's up to Jack to evacuate the townsfolk and save the day!

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead
programme.desc: Night of the Living Dead opens with chaos - the brains of the recently deceased have become mysteriously reanimated, causing the dead to rise and feed on human flesh. While visiting their father's grave, Barbra (Judith O'Dea) and her brother Johnny (Russell Streiner) are attacked by a strange shuffling man. Barbra runs into a farmhouse where she encounters Ben (Duane Jones), and they must find a way to save themselves from the horde of zombies. George Romero's Night of the Living Dead is hailed as one of the most influential horror films of all time - inspirational, thought provoking, and most importantly, terrifying!

programme.title: Attack of the Giant Leeches
programme.desc: Massive bloodsucking monsters are at large in this Drive-in schlock classic from producers Roger and Gene Corman. The legendary Yvette Vickers (Attack of the 50-foot Woman) stars along with Jan Shepard, Ken Clark, and some nasty giant leeches! Basically, they're just a bunch of actors in plastic costumes, but director Bernard Kowalski (Hot Car Girl, Night of the Blood Beast) made the most of his small budget. When the Cormans wouldn't hire a crew to push the camera raft while filming water scenes in the Pasadena Arboretum, Kowalski put on a bathing suit and did it himself!

programme.title: Blades
programme.desc: Somewhere in New Jersey, a peaceful country club becomes the hunting ground for a demonically possessed lawnmower with a taste for human flesh.

programme.title: Nightbeast
programme.desc: An alien with an appetite for blood and disembowelment is set loose on a small town when its spacecraft crashes and explodes. When sheriff Jack Cinder and the local militia arrive on scene to investigate, a violent battle ensues, leaving only Jack, his sensuous lady deputy and a wild-eyed redneck and survivors. With a corrupt mayor unwilling to stand up against the Nightbeast's reign of carnage and destruction, it's up to Jack to evacuate the townsfolk and save the day!

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Simon has invented an incredible virtual reality experience that allows him to join in on his favorite films ... but what happens when the virtual reality becomes Simon's actual reality?

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: After the death of a loved one, a drinking habit becomes insatiable. Then, dolls are Evie's favorite companions, but when a severed toy head appears in her dollhouse, Evie can no longer protect them.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A platoon of American soldiers finds something more sinister than war behind enemy lines. Then, a man with a passion for found objects finds something particularly odd: a finger ... and it's growing.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Teens who go trick-or-treating are met with fear instead of smiles and sweets. Then, Justin brings home the wrong suitcase and is shocked to find a living man stuffed inside.

programme.title: Creepshow

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A killer gets stranded in a funeral home with a mortician and his monkey paw. Then, former Mayor Barkley is in prison, and with his most loyal supporters locked up too, his odds don't look good.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Dr. Sloan discovers a cure to weight loss in a rare fat-sucking leech. Then, Rose believes she found Champ, the legendary monster of Lake Champlain, but the real monster may still be out there.

programme.title: A Creepshow Animated Special
programme.desc: A surgeon crash-lands on a desert island ... how strong is his will to survive? Then, a teenager's family vacation becomes even more torturous when they pull off the road to see a sinister circus.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. He gets nowhere with Jethro, so Pearl comes and she thinks he is testing her virtue when he tries to lay her on his couch.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts in Court" - When a couple who ran into the Clampett's car discover that they're rich, they fake injuries and sue the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Entertain" - Jed arranges a party to get Grannie out of her doldrums and invites the Drysdale. But when Mr. Drysdale's boss comes and wants to meet them for dinner, he wonders if he's going to be sent to an Alaskan bank when his boss finds out he's been lying about Jed and his family being sophisticated.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Becomes a Father" - Mlle. Denise comes back for the birth of her dog's puppies, and to see Jed. They do some "courtin' and sparkin'" Mrs. Drysdale isn't nearly so happy when she finds the puppies share more in common with Duke the bloodhound than Claude the poodle.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts and the Dodgers" - Jed and Jethro go golfing with Leo Durocher, coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers, who wants to recruit Jethro as a pitcher when he sees how well he can throw.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Pays His Income Tax" - When an IRS agent gets chased away by Granny, Mr. Drysdale tells him the story of how the Clampetts came to be rich and move to Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Spring Tonic" - Miss Hathaway's brilliant-but-frumpy bank protegee Gloria is really a sultry gold-digger with eyes for Jed's $34 million, and Jed's just had his annual dose of Granny's spring tonic.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Cuts the Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale continues to be forced into doing "practices from the past" by Mrs. Smith-Standish, who discovers that Jed's family is possibly the first to come the country. If so they'd be famous worldwide, but does Jed want that?

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: New Mexico
programme.desc: What happens when Abraham Lincoln himself comes to New Mexico to discuss a treaty with Native American chief Acoma (Ted de Corsia)? Can the hotbed of emotions be quieted by cavalry Captain Hunt (Lew Ayres)? Or will the Captains superiors insist on provoking with the native tribe? Find out what happens in this fictional tale packed with action from 1951!

programme.title: Enemy of the Law
programme.desc: Tex Ritter stars in this western drama about a man who goes undercover to befriend a convict believed to have stolen a shipment of government gold. Also starring Guy Wilkerson as Panhandle Perkins, this offering from Hollywoods Poverty Row features the usual fistfights and entertaining storyline that made these modestly budgeted features well worth watching. Songs include Teach Me To Forget and You Will Have to Pay both sung by Tex!

programme.title: Heartland
programme.desc: A widow with a young daughter travels to a ranch in Wyoming to manage the household of a rancher. After a while the man and woman develop a relationship that leads to a marriage. But life in the harsh place takes its toll.

programme.title: Hurricane Horseman
programme.desc: Pancho Gomez kidnaps Don Roberto and his daughter, Tonita for ransom. Can an undercover lawman named Gun Smith save the pair, or will he be caught himself?! Smith gets a job in Gomez's camp supposedly to repair their guns. Instead he fixes the guns so they will misfire. When Gomez refuses to let him go, he sends his horse for the Sheriff! This campy western from 1931 stars Lane Chandler and is sure to keep you enthralled from beginning to end. Directed by Armand Schaefer; starring Marie Quillan and Walter Miller.

programme.title: Six Gun Trail
programme.desc: Legendary cowboy star Tim McCoy stars as Captain William Carson, who along with his sidekick Magpie does battle with jewel thieves. Its an actionfilled drama of the West, with plenty of blazing guns and heman adventure! There are a couple of songs, too, including Moon Over the Plains but they arent sung by Tim McCoy. He was too darned tough to sing! Also starring Nora Lane, Ben Corbet; directed by Sam Newfield.

programme.title: The Star Packer
programme.desc: He Dared Death In The Outlaw's Lair! John Travers (John Wayne) takes on a mysterious gang leader known as The Shadow in this vintage western from Hollywoods Poverty Row. Director/writer Robert N. Bradbury (Blue Steel, Riders of Destiny) gets the best of a remarkable cast that includes George Gabby Hayes. Of interest to film buffs is that the feature was shot in various California locations by Oscar winning cinematographer Archie Stout (The Quiet Man).

programme.title: Doc Holliday
programme.desc: Doc Holliday runs a small dental practice in rural Georgia when he is visited by Wyatt Earp and offered a chance to become a United States Deputy. All Doc has to do is capture the Bridle Brothers - two of most notorious outlaws the West has ever seen. programme.title: Strongman Champions League

programme.title: Strongman Champions League

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: This week is a good one, can you feel the energy? Sit still, just look at me and try and feel the energy, because I can tell you, the atmosphere here is electric, it’s explosive, there is a pure elemental force going through these athletes' veins. I’m shaking because it’s Poland, it’s powerful, it’s strongman champions league, and you know what I'm going to say, get ready because it’s about to get heavy.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon grills a spatchcock chicken with avgolemono sauce in his Venice Beach front yard, joined by his wife Liz and co-host Liv. The episode features a lavender lemon drop martini and explores grill techniques for perfectly crispy, juicy poultry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall takes center stage during “Home Week” as she introduces Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly to a nostalgic Nashville staple: the hot fish sandwich. Drawing from her childhood memories, Carla explains that unlike the city’s famous hot chicken, this dish is all about being served piping hot straight from the oil rather than spicy. As she prepares cornmeal-crusted catfish, she shares stories of visiting local spots like Ed’s Fish, where the sandwich was a simple, affordable treat enjoyed on Fridays and often eaten outdoors. The hosts dive into a lively conversation about how Nashville has transformed over the years, balancing its old-school charm with its modern “Nash Vegas” energy, while also reflecting on how food traditions connect to community and shared experiences. Carla walks through the cooking process step-by-step, offering practical tips on frying, seasoning, and building the perfect sandwich with white bread, yellow mustard, hot sauce, pickles, and onions. Once assembled, the sandwich sparks an enthusiastic reaction, with the hosts praising its comforting, craveable simplicity. The conversation expands into broader themes about slowing down, engaging with others, and the lost art of waiting for food. The episode continues with fan mail, where the hosts share personal stories about Easter traditions, hidden talents, and the nuances of giving honest feedback. A playful hometown trivia game rounds things out, followed by a humorous and eclectic giveaway from Carla’s own closet. The episode closes on a high note, celebrating the joy of food, memory, and connection

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli, the chef-owners behind the celebrated New York restaurant Don Angie, for a lively episode dedicated to Italian American comfort food. The conversation begins with admiration for the restaurant’s iconic lasagna before shifting to the main event: a deeply flavorful, “life-changing” meatball recipe inspired by a blend of both chefs’ grandmothers’ traditions. As Angie and Scott walk through their process, they share chef-driven techniques like using a milk-soaked panade for moisture, puréed onions for even flavor distribution, and roasted garlic to avoid harsh raw bites. The hosts jump in with questions and personal anecdotes, sparking a broader discussion about Italian versus Italian American cooking, rule-breaking in the kitchen, and regional food identity—from Cleveland to New York. The group also plays a spirited round of “Discuss,” debating topics like garlic usage, cheese on seafood, and proper spaghetti etiquette, leading to humorous and opinionated exchanges. Along the way, they highlight the philosophy behind Don Angie’s food: bold, unapologetic flavors built on cheese, garlic, and tradition—without strict adherence to old-world rules. As the meatballs finish baking, they’re tossed in a simple, bright tomato sauce and served family-style, earning rave reviews from the hosts for their rich texture and layered flavor. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway of the chefs’ cookbook and a final celebration of Italian American cuisine, emphasizing creativity, collaboration, and the joy of cooking without limits.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome legendary pitmaster Melissa Cookston for a lively, flavor-packed episode centered around Mexican-inspired smoked chicken wings. The conversation kicks off with playful banter about grilling, live-fire cooking, and Melissa’s impressive barbecue credentials, setting the stage for a masterclass in flavor. Melissa walks the hosts through her approach to wings, emphasizing dry rubs over heavy sauces and showcasing a spice blend inspired by her travels to Mexico, featuring garlic, onion, smoked Hungarian paprika, ancho chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Along the way, the group dives into deeper cooking philosophy—trusting your instincts, cooking with your senses, and understanding that great barbecue is about feel as much as technique. Melissa shares insights on smoking temperatures, wood choices, and why over-smoking can ruin a dish, comparing it to over-salting. The energy stays high as Clinton hilariously spirals into wing-induced chaos, while Carla and Michael debate everything from condiments to air fryers. The episode also highlights Melissa’s new cookbook, Fanning the Flames, described as a love letter to her global culinary travels and the people who inspired her. After cooking, the group digs into the wings, followed by a round of “Chew It or Screw It” and fan mail, blending humor with heartfelt moments. The episode wraps with giveaways and a celebration of community, creativity, and the joy of cooking with fire.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this heartfelt Mother’s Day episode of Chewed Up, Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly reflect on the women who shaped them while Carla leads the kitchen with a show-stopping mimosa brioche French toast topped with frangipane. Inspired by her mother’s love of simple breakfast classics like pancakes and French toast, Carla elevates the dish into a baked, brunch-worthy centerpiece featuring brioche, apricot jam, almond-rich frangipane, and a champagne-orange glaze. As the dish comes together, the hosts share nostalgic stories about their childhoods—from weeknight dinners and Sunday sauces to special outings at places like Bob Evans and Chinese restaurants—highlighting how food connects to family memories. The conversation flows with humor and warmth, touching on generational differences in cooking habits and dining culture. Once the dish is baked to golden perfection, the trio digs in, praising its rich texture and balance of flavors, with Carla’s creative twist winning everyone over. The episode continues with playful segments like “What Would Mom Say,” where the hosts share funny and revealing lessons from their mothers, followed by fan mail and a casual food discussion. The show wraps with a giveaway of Carla’s personal artwork and a sincere tribute to moms everywhere, celebrating the love, lessons, and meals that define family traditions.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in the kitchen as he prepares a nostalgic Quiche Lorraine inspired by his mother, making the case for a simple yet meaningful Mother’s Day brunch made at home. As he walks through the process—layering raw onions, crispy bacon, and a rich egg-and-cream custard into a par-baked crust—Carla Hall and Michael Symon chime in with their own takes on brunch traditions, ingredient preferences, and the beauty of regional cuisine. The trio shares laughs over everything from vintage baby names to the infamous “Real Men Don’t Eat Quiche” era, using humor to dismantle outdated ideas around food and masculinity. Along the way, they highlight the importance of texture and balance, celebrating the silky, slightly jiggly custard as the hallmark of a perfectly baked quiche. A fan interaction segment adds a personal touch, with a “Fan of the Day” selected for a custom video message, reinforcing the show’s community-driven spirit. Once the quiche comes out of the oven, the hosts dig in, offering real-time reactions and thoughtful observations about flavor, technique, and versatility—debating whether quiche is best suited for brunch, lunch, or dinner. The episode wraps with a heartfelt nod to mothers everywhere and a reminder that sometimes the simplest dishes, made with care, are the most memorable.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon takes a nostalgic trip back to his early culinary days, recreating an OG state fair classic—steak on a stick—while Carla Hall and Clinton Kelly dive into a lively, humor-filled conversation about food, memories, and the joy of eating with your hands. Using flank steak, Michael walks through his simple but flavor-packed marinade of soy sauce, Worcestershire, Dijon, and spices, explaining the importance of cutting against the grain and letting the meat soak overnight for maximum tenderness. As the skewers hit the grill, the hosts debate whether food is inherently more fun on a stick, with Clinton firmly in the “yes” camp and Michael offering a more chef-driven perspective. The conversation branches into strong opinions on kebabs, with the trio agreeing that mixing vegetables and meat on the same skewer is a mistake, leading to soggy results. Along the way, they share personal anecdotes—from Carla being mistaken for Tracee Ellis Ross to Michael’s frustrations with diners’ rigid expectations around classic dishes—highlighting the humor and unpredictability of food culture. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting the finished steak, praising its bold flavor and simplicity, while also announcing a fan giveaway tied to Michael’s Simply Symon show. As always, the group signs off with their signature blend of warmth, wit, and encouragement to keep cooking and having fun in the kitchen.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall takes the lead in the kitchen as she introduces a playful and unexpected dish: fried Kool-Aid, inspired by nostalgic state fair treats from her childhood. As she walks through the process of making the vibrant red dough using Kool-Aid powder, flour, sugar, and a leavening agent, Clinton Kelly reacts with skepticism while Michael Symon shares his own less-than-great experience trying the dish at a fair. The conversation quickly turns humorous as Carla recounts a bizarre childhood memory of having a runny nose at the fair, prompting playful teasing from the group. As the dough is fried into donut hole-like bites, the hosts discuss technique, including how the dough naturally flips in hot oil and how to tell when it’s perfectly cooked. Carla elevates the dish by tossing the warm bites in lemon sugar and offering a Kool-Aid glaze for dipping, balancing sweetness with a bright citrus note. Once tasted, both Clinton and Michael are pleasantly surprised, praising the light texture and more refined flavor compared to traditional fair versions. The episode transitions into a lively fan mail segment where the hosts debate their least favorite vegetables and share personal preferences, followed by a special “fan of the day” moment celebrating a crew member’s mother’s birthday with jokes and a spontaneous song from Clinton. The episode wraps with the hosts encouraging viewers to engage with the show and teasing future fun.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes on a true first in his cooking career by attempting homemade corn dogs without any prior test run, bringing a mix of nerves and excitement to the kitchen as Michael Symon and Carla Hall cheer him on. Inspired by a popular recipe from Natasha’s Kitchen, Clinton walks through the batter and frying process in real time, navigating hot oil, technique adjustments, and plenty of playful banter. As the trio discusses their favorite fair foods—from funnel cake to childhood carnival memories—the conversation veers into hilarious territory, including rollercoaster mishaps and food-related regrets. Despite the pressure, Clinton successfully executes the corn dogs, achieving a golden, airy batter that impresses both Carla and Michael. The tasting confirms the dish as a win, with praise for its balanced sweetness, texture, and classic flavor. The episode also features a “Fan of the Day” segment, celebrating Charlene from New York with a personalized shoutout, along with a quick fan mail discussion about music preferences while cooking, revealing everything from Judy Garland to Metallica and R.E.M. The episode wraps on a high note, with Clinton’s confidence restored, the group celebrating a successful first attempt, and the hosts encouraging viewers to engage, subscribe, and bring the fun into their own kitchens.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon takes over the kitchen to teach essential knife skills and prep techniques that can instantly improve anyone’s cooking game. Joined by Clinton Kelly and Carla Hall, the trio dives into the importance of sharp knives, proper sharpening methods, and why most home cooks only really need three knives in their kitchen arsenal. Michael demonstrates how to properly hold a chef’s knife, sharpen blades using a wheel sharpener, and explains the difference between honing and sharpening, while Clinton enthusiastically jumps in with detailed play-by-play commentary and cooking science facts. Carla keeps the conversation lively with jokes about Michael’s long-winded explanations and practical reminders about time-saving kitchen habits. Throughout the episode, Michael walks viewers through slicing celery, chiffonading herbs, and properly dicing onions while explaining the safest and most efficient cutting techniques. The hosts also passionately debate cutting boards, with Michael launching into a hilarious rant against plastic, ceramic, and glass boards in favor of traditional wood boards. Humor runs throughout the segment, including a conversation about Ginsu knives, microplastics, and Clinton’s grammar-school definition of “interjections.” The episode wraps with the “Fan of the Day” segment, where Lysa from Seattle wins a personalized video message from the hosts, followed by a final round of playful insults and banter before signing off.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall shares her ultimate biscuit-making secrets while teaching two different techniques for creating perfect homemade biscuits. Joined by Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly, Carla walks through her trusted biscuit recipe while revealing the small adjustments and baking tricks she has collected over years of cooking, tasting, and learning from other biscuit makers. The conversation centers around how technique can completely transform the same recipe, as Carla demonstrates both drop biscuits baked in muffin tins and laminated layered biscuits made through folding and stacking dough. Along the way, the hosts discuss the importance of cold butter, the role of steam in creating flaky layers, and why full-fat buttermilk makes a major difference in texture and tenderness. Michael and Clinton enthusiastically jump into the science behind baking while keeping the conversation playful, debating butter pockets, crispy edges, and the superiority of muffin-tin stuffing. Carla also shares clever practical tricks, including using oil instead of excess flour on the work surface and flipping biscuit dough for better height and rise. The trio reflects on how cooking competition shows continue to teach even experienced chefs new techniques, reinforcing the episode’s theme that cooking is a constant learning process. After pulling both styles of biscuits from the oven, the hosts compare the textures and flavors of each version while slathering them with butter, jam, and honey. The episode wraps with Carol from Columbus, Ohio being selected as the “Fan of the Day,” earning a personalized message from the hosts before everyone signs off stuffed and satisfied.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon put eight different store-bought salsas to the ultimate taste test in a bracket-style showdown to determine which salsa deserves the top spot. The trio samples everything from chunky bean-and-corn varieties to smoky roasted garlic blends and fiery chipotle-forward options while debating what makes a great salsa, including texture, freshness, acidity, spice level, and chip compatibility. Along the way, the hosts joke about grammar corrections, “street cred,” and Michael’s love of spicy food while offering surprisingly detailed flavor breakdowns. Carla picks up floral notes in one salsa, Michael compares another to Fruity Pebbles cereal, and Clinton passionately defends smoother, sauce-like salsas that would work well on enchiladas. As the competition narrows, the hosts discover that their favorites lean toward bold, chef-driven flavor profiles featuring cumin, roasted garlic, and layered chile heat rather than basic restaurant-style salsa. Michael gravitates toward the spiciest contender while Clinton and Carla favor a cumin-forward salsa that feels homemade and versatile enough to cook with. The episode wraps with a cheerful “Fan of the Day” segment celebrating Lisa from Detroit before the hosts sign off.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly take on one of the internet’s most overhyped dishes — avocado toast — and each put their own spin on the trendy classic. Michael keeps things fresh and herb-forward with mashed avocado, citrus, chili flakes, dill, mint, chives, and crunchy sea salt while sharing his philosophy on cooking intuitively and using whatever is in the fridge or garden. Clinton recreates a nostalgic avocado and grapefruit salad from the 1980s, layering honey-drizzled grapefruit, poppy seeds, and red onion over avocado toast for a sweet and savory twist. Carla keeps hers simple and flavorful with fresh herbs and crispy shallots. Along the way, the hosts debate food trends, influencer culture, protein obsession, anchovies, and the infamous “avocado hand” kitchen injury, leading to plenty of playful bickering and laughs between Clinton and Michael. The show wraps with the trio agreeing that there’s no single right way to make avocado toast — it’s all about personal taste and creativity in the kitchen.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in a conversation all about meal prepping and healthy breakfast habits while showing Carla Hall and Michael Symon his simple overnight oats recipe. Clinton explains how prepping breakfast the night before has helped him make smarter food choices and stay on track with his health goals, sharing that he has lost 14 pounds through portion control and calorie awareness without giving up the foods he loves. As he combines oats, oat milk, and chia seeds into mason jars, the hosts joke about everything from “wet walnuts” and frozen yogurt nostalgia to chia pets and old-school diet culture. Carla adds her perspective on whole foods and how certain ingredients help keep people full longer, while Michael balances the conversation with humor and practical advice about calories, exercise, and understanding how different foods affect the body. The discussion expands into smart scales, fitness misconceptions, and the importance of focusing on overall wellness instead of obsessing over a single number on the scale. Later in the episode, the hosts answer fan mail questions about making homemade pet food and whether they prefer performing in front of live audiences or taped studio environments, reflecting on their experiences filming at food festivals and live events. The episode wraps with the announcement of the Fan of the Day and sponsor mentions for Fresh Direct and Frontgate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon revisits one of the most important dishes of his career as he makes his famous rotisserie chicken mac and cheese, a creamy pasta recipe that first became a hit at his Cleveland restaurant Piccolo Mondo in 1992. Using rigatoni, heavy cream, goat cheese, fresh rosemary, and shredded rotisserie chicken, Michael walks Clinton Kelly and Carla Hall through the deceptively simple recipe while sharing stories from his early days as a 22-year-old executive chef running a packed live-fire kitchen. The conversation turns nostalgic as Michael explains how the dish followed him from restaurant to restaurant and became such a customer favorite that he eventually had to remove it from the menu. The hosts laugh over Michael’s son Kyle unknowingly eating goat cheese for years despite insisting he hated it, while Clinton passionately defends rosemary after recalling a social media argument about the herb. The episode also features sponsor shout-outs to Fresh Direct and Frontgate, plus playful banter about portion sizes, “fat soup diets,” and airplane etiquette after eating rich pasta dishes. Once plated, the hosts rave about the comforting flavors, especially how the chicken melts into the creamy sauce and how the rosemary balances the tanginess of the goat cheese. The episode wraps with the selection of Melicia from New York as the “Fan of the Day,” followed by more joking between the hosts as they finish off the rich and nostalgic dish.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: In the debut episode of Simply Symons, Michael Symon welcomes viewers into his Venice, California front yard alongside his wife Liz Symon and their friend Olivia Annacone Cisic for an unfiltered, laid-back cookout. Celebrating Cinco de Mayo, Michael prepares simple yet flavorful black cod fish tacos on a plancha, walking through his approach to seasoning, cooking fish by sight, and building a bright, crunchy slaw with cabbage, citrus, and charred poblanos. The conversation flows easily as the trio shares cooking tips, debates ingredients like corn versus flour tortillas, and jokes about knife sharpening habits, cilantro preferences, and home cooking realities. Liz complements the meal with a refreshing jalapeño margarita, balancing heat with fresh lime juice, simple syrup, and a hint of orange liqueur, while adding her own creative twist with lemon and bitters. Throughout the episode, the group emphasizes flexibility in cooking—encouraging viewers to adjust flavors, garnishes, and spice levels to their own tastes. Lighthearted banter continues as they assemble tacos, discuss plating ideas, and highlight optional additions like crema and avocado. The episode wraps with a taste test, enthusiastic reactions to both the tacos and cocktail, and a reminder that great food doesn’t have to be complicated—just fresh, simple, and made with joy.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime: Niku Udon; Lemongrass-Caramel Chicken; Stir-Fried Angel Hair Pasta - Chris is in the kitchen to take you through three winning recipes from his rotation: A comforting udon noodle dish with thin slices of beef in a smoky, savory, slightly sweet broth; simple braised/stir-fried chicken that is somewhere between Vietnamese clay pot catfish and Taiwanese Three Cup Chicken; and a go-to quick pasta with tofu and veggies stir-fried in a flavor-packed sauce.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Fluffy, Buttery Biscuits for the Kiddos: Let's be clear: These are not the biscuits that chef Joshua Skenes used to serve at his three-Michelin-star restaurant Saison. Have I had those biscuits? Yes. Have I watched Josh make biscuits? Yes. Do I have his recipe in my phone? Maybe. Do I glance at that recipe whenever I set out to make biscuits for my kids? Well, wouldn't you? Jokes aside, Josh's biscuits are world-class, and are a product of not just the recipe, but his products and experience. I'm never gonna get them exactly right, but the ones I make are inspired by his. They're definitely passable and my kids can take them in whichever direction they like. My daughter likes it savory. My son likes them smothered in jam.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Sunday Gravy All Week: We don't need to tell you that kids like spaghetti and meatballs. Who doesn't? And Sunday gravy—with its chunks of fatty meat and sausage bobbing around in red sauce with the meatballs—is even better. Now, the real prize is what else you can do with that gravy. It's not just for lasagna and eggplant parm. Chris Ying shows you his Sunday Gravy recipe, and then how to take that same punchy, meaty, tomato-based umami sauce and use it to make beefy bean and cheese burritos and ratatouille.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Stuffed Cabbage (It's Hiding a Giant Meatball!): Sometimes you just need to wrap a delicious meatball in something that makes you feel like you're giving your kids a vegetable. Maybe your kids will reject the cabbage wrapper and leave for you to snack on, or maybe this is the one time that convinces them that cabbage is amazing? Either way, Chris Ying shows you another go-to recipe that is easy and flexible—seasoned meatballs wrapped in cabbage leaves and cooked in a delicious, tomato-y broth.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Cantonese-Style Steamed Fish with Morgan McGlone: World-class Australian chef Morgan McGlone joins Chris Ying to share his go-to technique for preparing a whole steamed fish—something his son absolutely loves! A lot of people are intimidated by cooking a whole fish, but this is our plea to you to get your kids started on steaming/eating whole fish early in life!

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Japanese Curry Your Kids (and You!) Will Love: Chris Ying shares his recipe for a creamy, dreamy Japanese curry that uses milk and honey to hit all the right notes that kids love. If your kids already like curry...you're golden with this recipe! And if your kids are new to the curry game, this is the perfect introduction.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime: Hacky Pad Thai for the Whole Family - This... is not pad thai. It has everything you love about pad thai—umami, sweetness, chewy rice noodles, brightness from lime—but this is the super hacky version I make for my wife and kids whenever I come across a package of fresh or semi-fresh noodles at the Asian market. If one of my Thai friends comes over, I'm hiding this from them. (If you're watching this, I'm sorry. It's all in the name of feeding the kids!) Ingredient-wise, you can keep tamarind and fish sauce in the fridge and pork belly in the freezer, but this recipe really depends on whether or not you have the noodles, which aren't available everywhere. You can use the dried kind, but I don't prefer them because they never seem to get to the right texture.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime: Quick Chicken Piccata - So far, my kids have always happily eaten this chicken piccata. It's crispy, sweet, sour, and super savory thanks to those little capers (which are easy to pluck out if the kids don't want them). But the real win is that my wife is obsessed with this dish, too. Usually, I can have this on the table in under 25 minutes, start to finish, which is a huge win. Also, while I almost always prefer chicken thighs, this is one of the rare instances where white meat is just fine.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Chicken Pot Pie" - Chris makes a recipe for beginner chicken pot pie from Allrecipes.com, using leftover chicken and canned potatoes. Yes, potatoes from a can.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Creamy Gochujang Pasta" - Majordomo TV’s Chris Ying unlocks the mystery of the TikTok trend of creamy gochujang pasta and wonders if it’s actually just a bad substitute for vodka sauce.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques suggests that mastering simple skills and techniques will make your life easier and when you’re comfortable in the kitchen, you’ll be more creative and avoid the trap of a quick fix with a frozen meal…or worse! He teaches you how to sharpen knives, cut and prepare vegetables and eggs and finally make a delicious gratin.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacues knows that most of us want to eat a healthy diet with lots of organic produce. Now so much is available at farmers markets and in grocery stores, you don’t have to spend a fortune if you chose what’s in season when it tastes the best. But for those times you’re in a hurry, it’s okay to grab prewashed, precut or frozen vegetables to make your life easier.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques shows that with a well-stocked pantry and a few good cooking techniques, you can make a tasty dish or a simple meal. His pantry essentials include potato flakes for a simple soup, spaghetti for a quick main dish, lentils, canned salmon, polenta and finally, in the freezer, a poundcake that can be transformed into a celebration dessert.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Gathering with family and friends around the table or for a game of boules is the life’s greatest pleasure for Jacques. He makes chicken wings, cucumber soup, merguez sausage, zucchini canapes, hummus and his father’s Fromage Fort. Simple food that can be made ahead means that you can enjoy a glass of wine with your company.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: When the weather heats up it’s time for family dishes that work on the grill or in the pan. Jacques' great summer menu with Asparagus in Butter Sauce, a Grilled Steak with Zucchini, topped with Anchovy Butter, Curly Dogs for the kids. He shows you classic ways to cut potatoes and ends with a Patriotic Raspberry and Blueberry Galette.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques celebrates birthdays, anniversaries and holidays with special meals. It’s when he splurges on caviar, oysters, even truffles–the ultimate extravagance. These occasions are a time to gather together around the table with family and friends and indulge in our favorite foods and wines.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques doesn’t always eat dessert at home, but when he does, it’s usually some kind of seasonal fruit. Classics like Baked Apple Bon Femme, Bananas Flambe, Cherry Compote or Pears in Puff Pastry… are served often. A new favorite is a British Lemon Posset. Other simple staples are Blueberries with Lemon and Mint or Grapefruit with Apricot Sauce, all great ways to end a meal.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati explores her Jewish roots with historian Mónica Unikel-Fasja, visiting historic sites in Mexico City. At Niddo, she cooks and reminisces with her sister, celebrating family, identity, and stories that shape a life.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Rústico in Cuauhtémoc for pastries with Iván and Germán Ortiz, then explores dreams and creativity at Casa Morgana in Roma with Kiren Miret. Tart, ice cream, and heartfelt stories celebrate passion and tradition.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati travels to Milpa Alta to harvest and char nopales with Chef Jorge Córcega, then joins Luis Alvarado’s family for their storied mole. Over a shared meal, they celebrate how tradition and flavor nourish the community.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Tlatelolco’s Gris with Gabriela Rojas to reflect on tragedy and hope, then learns danzón at Salón Los Ángeles with Miguel Nieto. With friends, they share chamorro de cerdo and toast resilience with creative micheladas.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati tours Mexico City’s changing skyline on the Iztapalapa Cable Bus, builds a bamboo bike at Bamboocycles, and enjoys tacos and tlacoyos, exploring how design, transit, and creativity shape a sustainable, connected city.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Xochimilco’s canals to see ancient chinampas, enjoys pipián verde with Chef Raúl, learns about axolotl conservation, and shares chile relleno with locals, celebrating resilience and tradition.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati traces Mexico City’s past with Erika Zúñiga, from Templo Mayor to Bellas Artes and a churro stop at El Moro. With Enrique Acevedo at Central de Abastos, they enjoy tacos de lengua and explore the city’s vibrant present and future.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits El Volador with Francisco Enríquez to see art for Day of the Dead parades. She connects with papel picado artist Yuriria Alfaro and honors tradition with fish tamales, celebrating imagination and heritage in Mexico City.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Chris and Molly add a master suite with laundry room to their home to match their expanding family. The homes existing plan has a fair amount space but it’s broken up and poorly utilized.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Find out how to make repairs to older windows and when to replace them, including how to replace window hardware, repair counterweights, unstick paint shut windows, make windows more energy efficient, and replace entire window units.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Danny shares some easy ways to get the maximum dollar on the resale of your home when updating a family room, including installing laminate flooring, adding crown molding, updating a fireplace, and replacing the entry door.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Danny returned to the Mississippi Gulf Coast to view the progress of rebuilding in the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina. He revisited homeowners whose houses were completely destroyed to offers advice for preparing for future natural disasters.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Every year, thousands of industry professionals gather to find out what’s next in kitchen and bath trends. This year the show unleashed everything from eco-friendly designs, retro ranges, lavish tubs, and innovative toilets.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: We’re creating more space for these homeowners to entertain with the addition of a game room. The game room flows incredibly well from the existing family room of the home and the vaulted ceiling makes it feel larger than it is.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Additions and amenities for outdoor entertaining have become very popular in recent years. Watch as we add a great space for outdoor entertaining on the back of this home.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Kitchen renovations are one of the most popular home improvement projects around. In this episode we’ll walk you through the 10 step process of revamping your kitchen and examine each of the decisions you’ll have to make along the way.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: These do-it-yourself home improvement projects cover everything from minor plumbing and electrical repairs to woodworking and laying ceramic tile. Remember, when using or installing any product, it is important to follow the manufacturers’ directions.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the first of our two-part Today’s Addition project, we’ll take you through the steps involved in adding a luxurious master bedroom addition, including planning, pouring the foundation, framing the walls and roof, and installing drywall.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: In the second of our two-part series on the master bedroom addition project, we complete the landscaping, wood flooring, and breakfast bar, and add some cool amenities including a video answering system and retractable screens.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: It’s easy to let home maintenance projects around the house slide, but if you don’t take care of them now, they can lead to much bigger problems down the road. Here are a few important weekend projects to put on your “to do” list.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: We’ve got eight great do-it-yourself ideas that are just right for weekend warriors, including building a fireplace mantel, a window seat, a backyard arbor swing, a stepping stone path, and a fabric covered window treatment.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: The bathroom we are renovating is about 50 years old and measures only 4’ wide by 9’ long. With the exception of the shower, all of the fixtures will be replaced, the floor tiled, new accessories added, and a vent fan mounted in the ceiling.

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Island Feast
programme.desc: Crete has a rich and complex culinary history, prompting Yotam to utilize his imagination and create unique variations on traditional regional dishes.

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Island Feast
programme.desc: Beginning in the exquisite fishing port of Bosa, Yotam prepares one of the Mediterranean's classic meals: freshly caught lobster on an open fire pit. Leaving the coast behind, he then travels into the rugged Sardinian heartland.

programme.title: Music Voyager
programme.desc: This is your chance to experience the rich culture of Sicily. Music Voyager travels into the heart of Sicily where we will dine with royalty and toast the region's famous vineyards amid the shadows of Mount Etna.

programme.title: Music Voyager
programme.desc: Travel the length of Italy's illustrious Amalfi Coast. From Positano to Capri, soak in the unique culture and cuisine of the region before ending with a private performance by a group of Tarantula musicians in a private wine cellar.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: In Perugia, we meet two women carrying on their family traditions of handmade stained glass and textiles, and we indulge in Baci candy. In Montefalco, Kathy tours the Tenuta Castelbuono winery, and enjoys a cooking lesson.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: Gran Caffe Gambrinus energizes us for our stay in Naples where we first visit the tailoring masters at Rubinacci. We then learn how to make a proper Neapolitan pizza, visit Mount Vesuvius, make handmade paper, go fishing, and much more.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane reveals the vast variety and robust flavors of the festive meze tradition of small plates of vibrant food meant to be shared. In Volos, she meets up with a Meze meister, and pushes the limits of meze at a cutting-edge restaurant.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Enjoy a picnic in a Naoussa vineyard where one noble grape dominates the table: the local Xinomavro. Learn how this delicious wine pairs with a wide variety of Greek cuisine, and how you can incorporate it into your family's Greek menu.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Lesvos is among the most vibrant and fascinating Greek islands, where the crossroads of history is exemplified by local dishes featuring the sea's bounty, eggplant, and Ouzo. Diane gets an insider's taste with a good friend and local son.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Experience the wonderful world of Greek cheeses. In Epirus, Diane follows feta cheese from its artisanal roots to modern production. We will also explore a range of other cheeses, visit a shepherd in Zagorohoria, and make farmhouse cheese.

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Island Feast
programme.desc: On the stunning island of Corsica, Yotam discovers how the local terrain, called the maquis, is infused in the flavors of the food when he meets with the island's most celebrated chefs and food producers who share the cuisine they love.

programme.title: Ottolenghi's Mediterranean Island Feast

programme.title: The Duel at Silver Creek
programme.desc: A gang of outlaws is murdering miners and taking over their claims, but they cross the wrong man when they target the father of Luke Cromwell (Audie Murphy), a gunslinger known as the Silver Kid. The local marshal, Lightning Tyrone (Stephen McNally), is also tracking the gang, but receives a crippling injury during a gunfight. Tyrone deputizes Cromwell, and the two take on the murderers together, while trying to resist the charms of Opal (Faith Domergue), the new beauty in town.

programme.title: Gunpoint
programme.desc: Near the Wild West town of Lodgepole, Colo., Sheriff Chad Lucas (Audie Murphy) gets shot during a train robbery -- not by the robbers, but by his jealous deputy, Cap. Hold (Denver Pyle), who believes he should be sheriff. Left to die, Chad rallies and goes off in search of the train robbers. Tracking them down in New Mexico, Chad and saloon owner Nate (Warren Stevens) chase after gang leader Drago (Morgan Woodward), who has taken Chad's ex-lover Uvalde (Joan Staley) as a hostage.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: Hired gun Reb Kittridge (Audie Murphy) goes to work in Montana for Matt Telford (Donald Randolph), who is looking to take over Dan Saxon's (Paul Kelly) fertile cattle land. What Reb doesn't bargain for is becoming a landowner himself after winning Saxon's property in a card game. Suddenly, he must work with Saxon and daughter Rita (Susan Cabot) to run the cattle to a rail line in order to make a mortgage payment. To stop him, Telford hires Reb's former partner, Johnny Lake (Charles Drake).

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more

Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: Hello Hello! - Super Simple Puppets and more

Moments later, little Billy skates right into the action and his skateboard becomes a gummy mess! Billy hurries to Carl’s Car Wash, but Carl won't clean his skateboard - it's a CAR wash, not a SKATEBOARD wash! Billy does some quick thinking...and his skateboard might just go through the car wash after all!

programme.title: 10 Little Dinosaurs - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: 10 Stegosauruses! 10 Triceratopses! 10 Pterodactyls! 10 Brontosauruses! And 10 Tyrannosaurus Rex! Wow! This Super Simple counting song introduces the numbers 1-10 and also some very cool dinosaur names! Can you say them all?!

programme.title: Steve's Street Sweeper - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Steve the Street Sweeper is cleaning the street when garbage splatters all over the side of his truck! He doesn't want a dirty truck, so Steve races to Carl’s Car Wash! With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Steve's vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Steve leaves one happy customer, and is ready to get back to sweeping rubbish.

programme.title: Down By The Bay - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see cat wearing a hat? How about a goat rowing a boat? Well you can in this video for the classic children’s song, Down By The Bay!

It's so gross, they don't even want to drive their van home to clean up. How can Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig share their love of food with their friends without cluttering their home? Mr. Monkey has a tasty idea! What is it? Watch and find out!

programme.title: Finny The Shark - Down In The Bay and more

programme.desc: Drive a car, ride a bike, row a boat, ride on a train, and fly an airplane with this playful and super fun song for kids; “Driving In My Car”! Driving in my car. Vroom vroom vroom. Driving very fast. Vroom vroom vroom. Driving very slow. Vroom vroom vroom. I like driving. Driving in my car.

programme.title: See You Later Alligator - Super Simple Puppets - Kids Songs - Super Simple

programme.title: Super Wings

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Novice Jibby, with Super Wings' help, lands safely in the Alps for Super Band's show. In Pixel-land, Alice and friends, aided by Super Wings, conquer challenges to enjoy fireworks.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Haruto's robot Metatron breaks down, activating villain Titan. Meanwhile, Jett and Marty build a rollercoaster and use magnets to reroute a stalled mile-long train.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Super Space Scouts and Jett, Matthew, and Dizzy chase a giant ball through a black hole. They save Earth from a moon-sized ball and deliver it to Mars.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Chloe and Dad escape a pterosaur world with a special glider after a time slip. Jett swaps roles, causing chaos with an inflatable duck, and learns teamwork.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Super Wings take Kamal and Nadia to the dinosaur era for a sports showdown, revealing all dinosaurs' strengths. In Scotland, they fix an inflatable playground for exhausted Nessie.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Lucie clean Amara's dad's underwater base and help an injured shark. Julian's safety gear interview reveals a crane's trouble and a forklift's heroics.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy help Louis with a chaotic tennis match and AI machine, leading to his success. Jett and Lucie recover a lost golden record from an alien spacecraft, sparking joy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: At Noah’s park, Camira hunts block dino eggs with Super Wings, facing surprises when Golden Girl snatches the prize and the egg escapes! Powered by new pod gear, Camira triumphs, while Jett braves the Sahara to find Latu and a fennec fox.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy jet to Jeju, helping Taehoon’s robot save Grandma’s shellfish from a giant fish, then team up to free it. On Mars, Jett and Marty chase Roy's lost bags, while Jinga dazzles with an out-of-this-world tour.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy join Hazel and Runi in Australia to help animals escape wildfires, then fly to Seoul where tiny Antny enters the Strongest Bug Contest, faces rampaging rivals, and, with Super Wings’ upgrades, helps save the city.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty help wash Maxy the giant plane and calm geysers at Oyunuma Pond, then deliver a Wright Flyer to Braden, sparking a time-hop to the Wright brothers, where teamwork lifts spirits and the Flyer takes flight.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Lucie, and Runi’s friendly contest becomes teamwork when they rescue toys from a Vietnam factory fire. Soon, Jett races to Dino World, freeing stranded friends and reuniting a T-Rex family as quakes rumble. Super Wings deliver!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Noah and the Dino Wings turn hail into fruit seeds, planting a thriving farm with Jett’s help and saving Dino World from a flood. Later, teamwork and a daring rescue inside Supersaurus bring the friends closer as everyone returns home safe.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Sicily, Jett and Curie foil invisibility pranks and stop a wild volcano eruption with Curie’s snow launcher, teaching Renata as chaos turns to learning. Aboard Ned’s ship, Super Electric Suits help them rescue everyone from a junk robot.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While the World Spaceport celebrates Jimbo’s retirement, Jett faces a UFO and Space Monster, piloting World Robot as chaos erupts. The team unites, time freezes, and Jimbo reveals Megamon is an old friend, choosing to stay with everyone.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and the Super Wings liven up Xiang Xiang’s Tanghulu shop, rescuing everyone from a syrupy mess and drawing big crowds. In Dino World, the Dino Wings halt a Dinobase takeover and reunite a wandering pterosaur hatchling with its mom.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Join our playful puppet host, Bard the Dragon, on a fun-filled adventure using everyday words such as flower, cat, and moon, and beautiful scenery from nature. World Of Words lets you and your little one share the excitement of discovering new words together!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Sharing the joys and traditions of the holiday season with your little one is truly one of the greatest gifts of parenthood, and Baby Santa's Music Box makes this magical time even more special! Celebrate by treating your baby and yourself to a captivating wonderland of bright lights, sparkling ornaments, sleigh bells, toys, winter scenes, jolly Santas and more -- all accompanied by beloved holiday melodies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: One of the greatest joys you'll ever experience is bonding with your baby during those calm, quiet moments. Lullaby Time: Soothing Sounds for Baby provides comforting sights and sounds as you relax and share this precious downtime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Your little one will want to keep a beat, snap his fingers and march his feet! Join new characters Pillie, the musical woodpecker, and Coco, the dancing cockatoo, and share the excitement of rhythm with your baby. World of Rhythm explores rhythm in music, rhythm in nature and rhythm as a form of expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Within the first year of babies' lives, their physical development begins to kick into high gear as they explore ways to physically interact with the world around them. BABY'S FIRST MOVES is a joyful celebration of their movement milestones -- including first steps, clapping, jumping, dancing and more.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby Mozart is an introduction to Mozart's beautiful music, which has been proven to have positive affects on people of all ages, especially babies. Images of brightly colored toys and known objects aid in the audio presentation of child-friendly arrangements and sound effects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Enjoy the best of summer, autumn and winter with Baby Einstein! Discover the familiar sights and sounds of the different seasons. Learn names of common objects associated with each through repetition and narration and see engaging images of whimsical toys that celebrate each. Discover the magic of each season from falling leaves, rolling beach balls and even snowmen.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover sign language along with your favorite Baby Einstein Characters! Learn how to sign the names of common things from different objects found at home and outside, family, and even your very own neighborhood through repetition and narration. Real-life images of toys and objects along with gentle melodies engage viewers as they learn a variety of different signs.

programme.title: Ben The Train Kids Nursery Rhymes
programme.desc: Ben the fun kids toy train is back with more songs and rhymes like old Macdonald, Alphabet song, phonics abc song, opposites song, incy wincy spider, five little monkeys, finger family etc. This family friendly children's show promotes learning and family bonds.

programme.title: Umi Uzi Halloween Songs for Kids

Nursery rhymes and children's songs are her favorite way of solving problems and learning new words. Join her this Halloween in this wonderful collection of the most popular scary songs specially made for kids!

programme.desc: Time for baby to take a bath! Bath time has never been so much fun! Enjoy this really fun kids song as baby takes his bath.

programme.title: Baby Bao Panda Non-Stop
programme.desc: Baby Bao Panda non-stop playlist with fun & kids song videos. Children will watch and learn our other popular & kids friendly songs like row row your boat, jack and jill, three little kittens, ten in the bed, incy wincy spider and more cartoon rhymes and songs for babies.

programme.title: Comedy Cartoon Videos - Deviled Eggz
